description: Choropleth map using false-colour infrared (FCIR) satellite imagery from Spot 5 (2.5 m resolution, 1/2/3 Band Combination), depicting an unspecified rural area, likely in France. The image source is CNES 2011©, Distribution Airbus DS/Spot Image. It illustrates different land cover types, with polygons outlined in yellow. Areas labelled "4100" are shown in shades of red-magenta and represent 4.1.0.0 Managed grassland. Areas labelled "2110" are shown in lighter pink-reds and represent 2.1.1.0 Non-irrigated arable land. A prominent dark blue/black river segment flows through the scene, bordered by both land cover types. Small greyish patches indicate built-up areas or settlements.
